response.text:
# Reflection

Household r0c3 is in a critical energy deficit situation: with only 0.27 kWh of 3 kWh capacity (9% SoC) and a 1.00 kW load while islanded, the battery will be depleted in approximately 16 minutes. The memory log shows a sustained pattern of incoming requests from r0c4 and r1c3 (both at ~28–32% SoC) and rejections from r0c2, which is prioritizing higher-urgency peers (r0c1, r1c2) in what appears to be a coordinated islanded microgrid crisis response. r0c3 itself is receiving rejections, indicating that neighbors recognize the system-wide scarcity and are rationing. With no established trust circles and no incoming energy offers, r0c3 must adopt a conservative posture: preserve remaining capacity for its own critical load, request energy with urgent priority to any available peer, and avoid sharing until SoC recovers above a safe threshold. The repeated policy parse failures suggest configuration instability; a simple, robust fallback is needed.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded microgrid in crisis; r0c3 SoC critically low (9%). Neighbors (r0c2, r0c4, r1c3) all requesting or rejecting; system appears to be rationing. No trust circles established. Prioritize own survival; request urgent aid from any peer. Do not share until SoC > 50%."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
